mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls reflects rising aggregate employment income, which drives consumption and corporate revenue expansion, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Months with positive month-over-month PAYEMS changes do not coincide with positive 20-day forward returns in greater than 55 percent of out-of-sample instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.0 percent signals restrictive monetary policy that elevates discount rates and debt service costs, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Months where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0 percent are followed by negative 20-day returns in fewer than 60 percent of triggered instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in industrial production and declines in the unemployment rate signal a broad-based recovery where output expansion aligns with labor market tightening, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint observations of rising INDPRO and falling UNRATE fail to generate positive 60-day returns with a rank information coefficient above 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a tight labor market, accelerating housing starts signal resource constraints and impending monetary intervention that raises discount rates, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 5.0 percent, month-over-month increases in HOUST are not followed by negative 20-day returns in greater than 55 percent of instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month industrial production decline exceeding 1.0 percent signals a sharp contraction in manufacturing output and corporate revenue, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Months with INDPRO declines exceeding 1.0 percent are followed by negative 5-day returns in fewer than 60 percent of triggered instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.0 percent signals robust aggregate demand expansion that supports corporate earnings re-rating, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarters with GDPC1 growth above 2.0 percent are followed by positive 60-day returns in fewer than 55 percent of instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of a federal funds rate above 4.0 percent and an unemployment rate above 5.0 percent signals a restrictive policy stance applied to a weakening labor market, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of FEDFUNDS above 4.0 percent and UNRATE above 5.0 percent does not produce negative 20-day returns in greater than 60 percent of triggered months over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts reflects synchronized consumer and construction sector strength, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint month-over-month increases in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 5-day returns with a rank information coefficient above 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.
